title: Symplectic embeddings from concave toric domains into convex ones
speaker: Dan Cristofaro-Gardiner
abstract: Embedded contact homology gives a sequence of obstructions to four-dimensional symplectic embeddings, called ECH capacities. These obstructions are known to be sharp in several interesting cases, for example for symplectic embeddings of one ellipsoid into another. We explain why ECH capacities give a sharp obstruction to embedding any "concave toric domain" into a "convex" one. We also explain why the ECH capacities of any concave or convex toric domain are determined by the ECH capacities of a corresponding collection of balls.

proof_stages.label: ECH-capacity obstruction landscape
proof_stages.summary: The talk situates four-dimensional symplectic embedding problems around ECH capacities, which are monotone obstructions known to be sharp in some benchmark cases and non-sharp in others.

proof_stages.label: Concave/convex toric-domain theorem
proof_stages.summary: Toric domains are defined from first-quadrant regions, the concave and convex subclasses are introduced, and the main theorem is stated: ECH capacities are sharp for embeddings from rational/general concave toric domains into convex ones.

proof_stages.label: Weight sequences and ball-packing reformulation
proof_stages.summary: A rational concave domain is decomposed by the weight-sequence procedure into a canonical ball packing, while a convex domain determines a larger ball whose complement is filled by balls, leading to an equivalent associated ball-packing problem.

proof_stages.label: Iterated blow-ups and sphere chains
proof_stages.summary: The symplectic blow-up is reviewed and then iterated, with small delta choices, to replace rational concave and convex toric-domain data by smooth blow-ups of CP2 containing prescribed chains of spheres.

proof_stages.label: Sphere-chain embedding criterion
proof_stages.summary: The desired embedding of the concave domain into the convex domain is reformulated as the existence of a symplectic form on CP2 blown up m+n times for which the two associated sphere chains embed with the required configuration.

proof_stages.label: Inflation and Taubes curve from ball packing
proof_stages.summary: Starting from a shrunken inclusion, the proof uses the assumed ball-packing data, Taubes Seiberg-Witten theory to find a suitable J-holomorphic curve, and symplectic inflation to enlarge the relevant sphere areas; rational restrictions are then removed by approximation.

proof_stages.label: Capacity sharpness conclusion
proof_stages.summary: Using monotonicity, the basic ECH-capacity axioms, and sharpness of ECH capacities for ball packings of a ball, the absence of ECH obstructions is converted into the associated ball packing and hence, by the preceding equivalence, into the required embedding.
